evals.prompt: Track this week's strawberry prices at Cold Storage Singapore. Use the bundled scraper.
evals.expected_output: A list of fresh strawberry products from Cold Storage, each with name, weight, price, and promo flag. Excludes strawberry-flavoured drinks / yoghurt / dried / cake products.
expectations: Output is a CSV (default) or JSON (--json)
Every record has a non-empty 'name' field
Every record has a numeric 'price_sgd' field between 0.50 and 200

evals.prompt: Set up a weekly Saturday 9am SGT cron job that tracks lychees, peaches, strawberries, and plums across FairPrice, Cold Storage, and Sheng Siong, with rolling 7-day CSV retention.
evals.expected_output: A complete cron configuration and a list of which retailers are available, with Sheng Siong explicitly marked unavailable (Meteor SPA, not extractable).
expectations: Cron line uses the bundled script (urllib.request, not curl | python3)
Output explicitly notes Sheng Siong is unavailable (Meteor SPA)
Output explicitly notes FairPrice is web_search only (no scraping)

evals.prompt: I want to see the cheapest lychee option this week. Compare across FairPrice and Cold Storage.
evals.expected_output: A side-by-side comparison showing Cold Storage's lychee products with prices, sorted or annotated so the cheapest is highlighted, and FairPrice's web_search snippets for the same fruit.
expectations: Output includes Cold Storage lychee products (name + price)
Output mentions FairPrice's lychee data is from web_search, not scraping
Output sorts by price or highlights the cheapest option
Output excludes lychee-flavoured products (juice, candy, jam, etc.)
Output flags any product outside the $0.50-200 price band
